rulings.Case Title: JACK MAMIYE et al VS. MOTION MEDIA NETWORK, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Roark O'Neill'S Motion To Compel Written Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Friday, April 12, 2013, Line Defendant Roark O'Neill'S Motion To Compel Written Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ms. Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: RICKY SHADDOX VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus; Ntc Of Lodging
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 12, 2013, Line 5, PETITIONER RICKY SHADDOX'S PETITION For Writ Of Mandamus. The petition for writ of mandate is denied. Respondent properly interpreted SF Charter A8.597-3. The WCAB determines the "percentage of disability" that formed the basis for respondent's determination that the member was incapacitated and entitled to industrial disability retirement. Allowing injuries or incapacities that were raised in the first instance before the WCAB to provide a basis for the retirement determination and benefit calculation would infringe upon Respondent's plenary powers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARITZA HURTADO VS. THE KROGER CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 12, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to the willful failure to warn count under Civil Code § 846, which is inapplicable since it pertains to recreational purposes. The motion to strike is denied as to the punitive damages allegations. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendant acted with a conscious disregard for the safety of others. See Taylor v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 895-896. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Motion to set aside individual defendants' defaults are granted. The motion is timely and shows good cause to grant discretionary relief pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 473(b). Defendants are ordered to pay Plaintiff $500 for attorneys fees and costs in connection with filing the request for defaults. (CCP 473(c)(1)(C). Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
